items.q: How was the Cuban Missile Crisis resolved?
items.correct: A secret deal: the USSR withdrew missiles from Cuba; the US publicly pledged not to invade Cuba and secretly agreed to remove Jupiter missiles from Turkey.
items.misconception: Kennedy stared Khrushchev down and the Soviets backed off unconditionally.
items.random_wrong: The crisis ended when Castro persuaded Khrushchev to withdraw.
items.why: The Turkey concession was kept secret for decades, fuelling the myth of unilateral US victory. The actual resolution was a negotiated compromise — important for understanding nuclear diplomacy.

items.q: Why did the Industrial Revolution begin in Britain?
items.correct: A combination of capital from trade and empire, coal and iron deposits, agricultural improvements, political stability, and a culture of invention.
items.misconception: Because James Watt invented the steam engine.
items.random_wrong: Because Britain banned slavery before other countries.
items.why: Inventions are effects of the conditions, not causes. Steam engines existed before Watt; what mattered was the economic and social environment that made them profitable to deploy at scale.

items.q: What were the long-term effects of the Black Death?
items.correct: Labour shortages strengthened peasants' bargaining power, weakened serfdom in England, contributed to the Peasants' Revolt, and reshaped religious authority.
items.misconception: The Black Death wiped out medieval society and ended the Middle Ages overnight.
items.random_wrong: The Black Death was caused by witches and was stopped by burning them.
items.why: The plague was catastrophic but medieval society continued. Its importance lies in slow structural changes (wages, labour, religion) — not in being a single 'ending' event.

items.q: On what date in 1972 did Bloody Sunday take place?
items.why: Correct answer: 30 January 1972. “12 July 1972” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which 1998 agreement established power-sharing in Northern Ireland?
items.why: It's Good Friday Agreement. “Sunningdale Agreement” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many civilians were killed by British soldiers on Bloody Sunday?
items.why: Exact answer: 13. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
